name: HealthEData_1Sandbox
title: Health eData 1 Sandbox
description: This is a **Healthedata1** sandbox for creation of resources and examples

resource.description: This example of a *US Core Appointment Profile* illustrates a booked appointment created in response to a referral ServiceRequest.
resource.name: Appointment From Referral Example

resource.description: This example of a *US Core Appointment Profile* illustrates a fulfilled appointment paired with the resulting Encounter that references it via `Encounter.appointment`.
resource.name: Appointment Resulting In An Encounter Example

resource.description: This is a booked future annual physical exam appointment example for the *US Core Appointment Profile*.
resource.name: Booked Future Appointment Example

resource.description: This is a cancelled office visit appointment example for the *US Core Appointment Profile*.
resource.name: Cancelled Appointment Example

resource.description: This is an encounter example for the *US Core Encounter Profile* that records the visit resulting from a scheduled appointment, referenced via `Encounter.appointment`.
resource.name: Encounter From Appointment Example

resource.description: This is a fulfilled follow-up office visit appointment example for the *US Core Appointment Profile*.
resource.name: Office Visit Follow-up Appointment Example

resource.description: This is a booked telehealth consultation appointment example for the *US Core Appointment Profile*.
resource.name: Telehealth Appointment Example

resource.description: The US Core Appointment Profile inherits from the FHIR [Appointment](https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/appointment.html) resource; refer to it for scope and usage definitions. This profile meets the requirements of the U.S. Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) *Appointment Data Element of the Encounter Information Data Class*. It sets minimum expectations for the Appointment resource to record, search, and fetch information about a healthcare event scheduled for a past, current, or future date and time. It specifies which core elements, extensions, vocabularies, and value sets SHALL be present in the resource and constrains how the elements are used. Providing the floor for standards development for specific use cases promotes interoperability and adoption.
resource.name: US Core Appointment Profile
